# Demo — CPU end-to-end on the smoke AOI

<!-- generated by: scripts/11_demo_timing.py (cache-warm run, 2026-06-12) -->

This is the reproducible CPU path: from a clone with a warm `data/cache/`, it
runs the whole pipeline on the 1 km² smoke AOI (`data/aoi/smoke.geojson`) in a
couple of minutes. There is **no single-command `demo`** — the demo *is* this
documented sequence, so every step is inspectable. The one GPU stage
(Prithvi-EO Burn-Scar inference) is **not** in this path; it is consumed as a
pre-baked COG already under `outputs/cogs/` (see
[`methodology.md`](methodology.md) and prompt `09_burn_scar_inference.md` for
the GPU reproduction route).

## Prerequisites

```bash
uv sync --locked --extra dev      # toolchain + deps (ruff/pyright/pytest live in the dev extra)
```

`data/cache/` warm with ETH-GCH, EFFIS, and DGT COSc (the static rasters the
score path consumes). On a **fresh clone** these are downloaded on the
`fetch-rasters` step (~600 MB) — that download dominates first-run wall-clock;
the timings below are **cache-warm**.

## Steps

```bash
# 1. Data-availability check (smoke AOI). YELLOW is expected — the 1 km² tile
#    is too sparse for the OSM class-count probe; any other non-zero is a stop.
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo audit --aoi data/aoi/smoke.geojson

# 2. OSM critical infrastructure → GeoParquet
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo fetch-osm --smoke

# 3. Static rasters → cache. Only the layers the score path consumes; the
#    species-level DGT COS ("cos") is future work and its DGT URL is 404.
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo fetch-rasters --smoke --only eth-gch,effis,cosc

# 4. ICNF Áreas Ardidas (burn perimeters) → GeoParquet
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo fetch-burns --smoke

# 5. Fuel-class layer (EFFIS + DGT COSc crosswalk) → COG
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo fuel-layer --smoke

# 6. Per-asset features + composite exposure rank → GeoParquet. --window-end
#    pins the score to the pre-baked smoke burn-scar COG's window so all six
#    features activate and the run is date-independent.
uv run wildfire-exposure-eo score --smoke --window-end 2026-06-09

# 7. Historical-burn validation report (lift / Spearman / ablation)
uv run python scripts/11_validate.py --smoke
```

## Timings (cache-warm)

| step | wall-clock |
|---|---:|
| `audit` | 42 s |
| `fetch-osm` | 62 s |
| `fetch-rasters` | 1 s |
| `fetch-burns` | 26 s |
| `fuel-layer` | 6 s |
| `score` | 8 s |
| `validate` | 0 s |
| **total** | **2.5 min** |

Well under the 30-minute CPU budget. Re-measure any time with
`uv run python scripts/11_demo_timing.py` (it runs the seven steps and prints
this table — every number here is its output). Network is required: Overpass,
ICNF ArcGIS REST, and the Microsoft Planetary Computer.
